title: Property Taxes and Homeowners Insurance After a Death
summary: Property-related bills don't pause during an estate process — keeping them current avoids serious complications later.
category: After a Loss III: Estate & Digital
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Property Taxes and Homeowners Insurance After a Death | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Property-related bills don't pause during an estate process — keeping them current avoids serious complications later.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Property taxes continue to accrue and must be paid regardless of probate status.
Homeowners insurance may need updating to reflect a vacant or transferring property.
Some insurers have specific "vacant home" policies if the property will sit empty.

paragraphs: Property taxes don't pause for probate or estate settlement — missed payments can lead to penalties or, eventually, tax liens, so continuing payments (often from estate funds, once accessible) is important throughout the process.

If a home will sit vacant for an extended period during probate, standard homeowners insurance may not fully cover it — some insurers require a specific vacant-home policy, worth asking about directly to avoid a coverage gap.

nextSteps: Confirm who will be responsible for ongoing property tax payments during the process.
Ask the insurer whether a vacant-home policy is needed.
Set a reminder for tax and insurance due dates throughout the estate process.
disclaimer: General information — the local tax assessor's office and the homeowner's insurer can confirm specific requirements.
